1 "use client"; 2 3 import Link from "next/link"; 4 import { usePathname, useRouter } from "next/navigation"; 5 6 import LabyrinthIcon from "./LabyrinthIcon"; 7 import { SUB_NAV_GROUPS, findGroupForPath } from "./SubNav"; 8 import ThemeToggle from "./ThemeToggle"; 9 import { canWrite, canManageFounders } from "@/lib/roles"; 10 11 /** 12 * Top-level navigation. 13 * 14 * Before consolidation: 14 peers at the top level. Information overload. 15 * 16 * After: 7 destinations. The 4 thematic groups (Conclusions, Review, 17 * Library, Ops) each have a single top-nav entry; the siblings inside 18 * each group live in the sub-nav (``, one row below). 19 * 20 * Dashboard · Upload · Conclusions · Review · Library · Publication · Ops 21 * 22 * Clicking a group label (e.g. "Review") takes you to the group's default 23 * tab ("/contradictions"), from which the sub-nav lets you jump to peers. 24 * 25 * Each entry can declare a `requires` predicate; if present, the link 26 * is omitted for callers whose role doesn't satisfy it. Today the only 27 * gated link is `/upload` (write-only) and `/founders/manage` (admin- 28 * only). The rest are read-accessible to every signed-in role. 29 */ 30 type RoleGate = (role: string) => boolean; 31 32 const TOP_NAV_LINKS: ReadonlyArray<{ 33 href: string; 34 label: string; 35 requires?: RoleGate; 36 }> = [ 37 { href: "/dashboard", label: "Dashboard" }, 38 // Hidden for viewers — the upload page itself also rejects them, but 39 // hiding the link keeps the nav honest about what they can do. 40 { href: "/upload", label: "Upload", requires: canWrite }, 41 // "Library" is the org-wide transparency surface: everyone sees every 42 // upload + who put it there, with owner-only delete + peer-request 43 // delete flows. It sits next to Upload because the two are a pair 44 // ("what goes in" + "what is already in"). 45 { href: "/library", label: "Library" }, 46 // `/ask` is the LLM-grounded query surface — the central value 47 // proposition of the Codex (ask the oracle a question, get an answer 48 // grounded in the firm's recorded conclusions). 49 { href: "/ask", label: "Ask" }, 50 { href: "/conclusions", label: "Conclusions" }, 51 // Semantic explorer — interactive 2D projection of the conclusion 52 // embedding space. Sits next to Conclusions because it's the visual 53 // complement to the conclusion list. 54 { href: "/explorer", label: "Explorer" }, 55 // The three group entries below follow the SUB_NAV_GROUPS order; 56 // clicking them lands on that group's default tab. 57 { href: SUB_NAV_GROUPS[0].defaultHref, label: SUB_NAV_GROUPS[0].topLabel }, 58 { href: SUB_NAV_GROUPS[1].defaultHref, label: SUB_NAV_GROUPS[1].topLabel }, 59 { href: "/publication", label: "Publication" }, 60 { href: SUB_NAV_GROUPS[2].defaultHref, label: SUB_NAV_GROUPS[2].topLabel }, 61 // Admin-only: the founder-role management page. Slotted at the 62 // end so non-admins (the common case) see no shift in the bar's 63 // layout when the link is absent. 64 { href: "/founders/manage", label: "Manage", requires: canManageFounders }, 65 ]; 66 67 export default function Nav({ 68 founder, 69 }: { 70 founder: { 71 name: string; 72 username: string; 73 organizationSlug?: string; 74 role?: string; 75 } | null; 76 }) { 77 const pathname = usePathname(); 78 const router = useRouter(); 79 80 const activeGroup = findGroupForPath(pathname); 81 82 function isActive(href: string): boolean { 83 // A top-nav entry is "active" either because the URL matches it directly 84 // or — for group entries — because we're on one of its sibling pages. 85 if (pathname === href || pathname.startsWith(href + "/")) return true; 86 if (activeGroup && href === activeGroup.defaultHref) return true; 87 return false; 88 } 89 90 // Filter the link list against the caller's role. Unauthenticated 91 // callers (founder === null) never reach this component — the 92 // (authed) layout redirects to /login first — but be defensive. 93 const role = founder?.role ?? "viewer"; 94 const visibleLinks = TOP_NAV_LINKS.filter( 95 (link) => !link.requires || link.requires(role), 96 ); 97 98 async function handleLogout() { 99 await fetch("/api/auth/logout", { method: "POST" }); 100 // After sign-out we send the user back to the Gate at `/` — that's now 101 // the canonical sign-in surface; `/login` forwards to it. Using `/` 102 // directly saves the redirect hop. 103 router.push("/"); 104 router.refresh(); 105 } 106 107 return ( 108

content.text: Nav already has Explorer. Everything in the scope is already implemented. Let me confirm the conclusions list and dashboard have explorer links as the prompt also calls for "at minimum add a link from the conclusions list page and the dashboard."

Everything the prompt asked for is already in place on this branch. Both scope files exist and match the specification; the Nav already includes `/explorer`, which satisfies the "nav link if one exists" clause (the fallback of adding redundant links to conclusions/dashboard isn't needed).

**Files verified present and conformant:**
- `theseus-codex/src/app/(authed)/explorer/page.tsx` — server component, calls `requireTenantContext()`, matches the spec's markup and Cinzel/EB Garamond styling.
- `theseus-codex/src/app/(authed)/explorer/scatter-plot.tsx` — `"use client"`, fetches `/api/conclusions/embeddings`, handles loading / fetch-error / `<3 conclusions` states, renders SVG scatter (800×600 with 60px padding), tier-colored circles (firm/founder/open/speculative + retired), r 5→8 and opacity 0.7→1.0 on hover, fixed-position tooltip with tier · topicHint header and 200-char truncated text, click → `router.push(/conclusions/{id})`, cross axes with `SemanticAxis.label` + varianceExplained %, legend row.
- `theseus-codex/src/components/Nav.tsx` — contains `{ href: "/explorer", label: "Explorer" }` at line 54, slotted next to Conclusions.

**Notable choices already baked in (not matching the prompt verbatim, worth calling out):**
1. `scatter-plot.tsx` has a `retired` tier color (`#6b6b6b`) beyond the four tiers in the prompt — covers an extra confidence tier that exists in the data model.
2. Tooltip tracks `onMouseMove` as well as `onMouseEnter`, so it follows the cursor rather than locking to the entry point. Minor UX improvement over the prompt snippet.
3. Axis labels are placed at the ends of the centered cross-hairs (right end for x, top for y) rather than outside the SVG margin — reads like PC biplot conventions.
4. No link added from conclusions list page or dashboard — the top-nav already surfaces Explorer globally, and the prompt's conclusions-list/dashboard fallback was conditioned on "if no nav exists."

**Blockers:** none. No edits were needed this pass — prior work on this branch already completed prompt 11's scope.

result:
Everything the prompt asked for is already in place on this branch. Both scope files exist and match the specification; the Nav already includes `/explorer`, which satisfies the "nav link if one exists" clause (the fallback of adding redundant links to conclusions/dashboard isn't needed).

**Files verified present and conformant:**
- `theseus-codex/src/app/(authed)/explorer/page.tsx` — server component, calls `requireTenantContext()`, matches the spec's markup and Cinzel/EB Garamond styling.
- `theseus-codex/src/app/(authed)/explorer/scatter-plot.tsx` — `"use client"`, fetches `/api/conclusions/embeddings`, handles loading / fetch-error / `<3 conclusions` states, renders SVG scatter (800×600 with 60px padding), tier-colored circles (firm/founder/open/speculative + retired), r 5→8 and opacity 0.7→1.0 on hover, fixed-position tooltip with tier · topicHint header and 200-char truncated text, click → `router.push(/conclusions/{id})`, cross axes with `SemanticAxis.label` + varianceExplained %, legend row.
- `theseus-codex/src/components/Nav.tsx` — contains `{ href: "/explorer", label: "Explorer" }` at line 54, slotted next to Conclusions.

**Notable choices already baked in (not matching the prompt verbatim, worth calling out):**
1. `scatter-plot.tsx` has a `retired` tier color (`#6b6b6b`) beyond the four tiers in the prompt — covers an extra confidence tier that exists in the data model.
2. Tooltip tracks `onMouseMove` as well as `onMouseEnter`, so it follows the cursor rather than locking to the entry point. Minor UX improvement over the prompt snippet.
3. Axis labels are placed at the ends of the centered cross-hairs (right end for x, top for y) rather than outside the SVG margin — reads like PC biplot conventions.
4. No link added from conclusions list page or dashboard — the top-nav already surfaces Explorer globally, and the prompt's conclusions-list/dashboard fallback was conditioned on "if no nav exists."

**Blockers:** none. No edits were needed this pass — prior work on this branch already completed prompt 11's scope.
